title: What is the pathway to citizenship: level 2
description: Test how well you can apply your knowledge of the pathway of citizenship!
topic_path: What is the pathway to citizenship?
What is the pathway to citizenship: level 2

assessment_items.question_content:
Najia’s family emigrated from Morocco after her dad got a job at Carnegie Mellon University. She and her parents have been residents in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania for the last fifteen years. She is now twenty-five years old, attending law school, and wants to become a citizen. 

**Does Najia qualify for naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; she is over the age of eighteen and has lived in the United States long enough to become a citizen.
Yes; she is a refugee, so she is automatically eligible for naturalization.
No; she has not lived in the United States long enough to be eligible for the naturalization process.
No; she does not have a job, so she does not meet the “good moral character” requirement.

assessment_items.question_content:
Pooja’s family immigrated to the United States from India three years ago. In those three years, she graduated from high school and started attending the University of Iowa. She’s nineteen now and wants to get American citizenship because it would make her eligible for a few more scholarships. 

**Does Pooja qualify for naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; she has not lived in the United States long enough to apply for citizenship.
No; she is not currently employed, so she is not eligible for naturalization.
Yes; she is over the age of eighteen, so she can apply for naturalization.
Yes; she has demonstrated that she is of “good moral character.”

assessment_items.question_content:
Isamara’s parents relocated her entire family from Bolivia when Isamara was nine years old. When their visa expired, Isamara’s parents stayed in the United States without authorization. Isamara has gone to public schools in Fort Wayne, Indiana since the fourth grade, so she can speak, read, and understand English. She’s eighteen and in eleventh grade. She’s taking US history, and she has an A in the class. 

**Does Isamara qualify for naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; she is an undocumented immigrant, so she can never apply for naturalization.
No; she could not pass the literacy test or the civics test required for naturalization.
Yes; she has lived in the United States for over five years, making her eligible for naturalization.
Yes; she is at least eighteen years old, which is the minimum age someone must be to apply for naturalization.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kyleigh immigrated from England when she was three years old. She’s lived in Lorain, Ohio for the past nine years, and she loves living in the United States. She learns that immigrants can become citizens if they go through the naturalization process in her eighth grade civics class. She decides she wants to become a naturalized citizen for her fourteenth birthday. 

**Does Kyleigh qualify for naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; she is not old enough to apply for naturalization.
No; she could not pass a literacy test for English.
Yes; she has lived in the United States for over five years.
Yes; she has enough knowledge to pass the US civics exam.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jermaine was born in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. He moved to the United States when he was four years old, when his mom got lawful permanent resident status and has lived in Flint, Michigan for the past fifteen years.  He is applying to universities in the fall and he wants to become a citizen before he starts college. He is a good student and has never gotten into any trouble with the law. He thinks he meets all the requirements to become a citizen.

**Does Jermaine qualify for naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; he has lived in the United States for long enough and is of “good moral character.”
Yes; he has a specialized skill that makes him eligible for an accelerated path toward citizenship.
No; he entered the United States at a young age, making him ineligible for naturalization.
No; he has not remained in the United States for the entire duration of his residency.

assessment_items.question_content:
Antoine’s family moved to Portland, Oregon, from France when Antoine turned thirteen. Antoine did not take the move well and began acting out. When Antoine was nineteen, he and a few friends broke into an electronics store and stole nearly \$5000 worth of technology, including laptops and phones. He served two years in prison for the crime. After Antoine got out of prison, he wanted to become a citizen to make a better life for himself. 

**Does Antoine qualify for naturalization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: No; he has not demonstrated that he is of “good moral character” making him ineligible for naturalization.
No; he immigrated to the United States as a child, so he cannot obtain citizenship.
Yes; he has lived in the United States for at least five years, the minimum requirement.
Yes; he is over the age of eighteen, so he can apply for naturalization.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rajesh moved his family from Bangladesh to Baton Rouge, Louisiana, seven years ago. He’s been a doctor at a Baton Rouge hospital for the last three years. He’s fluent in four languages, including English, and has taken to volunteering his time to provide medical care for other immigrants who have recently made their way to the United States. He’s about to turn forty and thinks it’s time for him to go through the naturalization process. 

**Does Rajesh qualify for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Yes; he has lived in the United States for at least five years as a lawful permanent resident.
Yes; he is technically an asylee, so he can automatically apply for naturalization.
No; he would not meet the language requirement for naturalization.
No; he has not worked for an American company for long enough to apply for naturalization.

assessment_items.question_content:
Najia’s family emigrated from Morocco after her dad got a job at Carnegie Mellon University. She and her parents have been residents in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania for the last fifteen years. She is now twenty-five years old, attending law school, and wants to become a citizen. 

**Which of the following qualifications does Najia already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She is at least eighteen years old.
She has been lawfully admitted to the United States.
She has lived in the US for at least five years.
She is currently enrolled at an American university.

assessment_items.question_content:
Pooja’s family immigrated to the United States from India three years ago. In those three years, she graduated from high school and started attending the University of Iowa. She’s nineteen now and wants to get American citizenship because it would make her eligible for a few more scholarships. 

**Which of the following qualifications does Pooja already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She meets the minimum age requirement.
She is of “good moral character.”
She has returned to India since immigrating.
She has lived in the United States for long enough.

assessment_items.question_content:
Isamara’s parents relocated her entire family from Bolivia when Isamara was nine years old. When their visa expired, Isamara’s parents stayed in the United States without authorization. Isamara has gone to public schools in Fort Wayne, Indiana since the fourth grade, so she can speak, read, and understand English. She’s eighteen and in eleventh grade. She’s taking US history, and she has an A in the class. 

**Which of the following qualifications does Isamara already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She meets the minimum age requirement.
She can meet the civics knowledge requirement.
She has lived in the United States for long enough.
She is a lawful permanent resident.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kyleigh immigrated from England when she was three years old. She’s lived in Lorain, Ohio for the past nine years, and she loves living in the United States. She learns that immigrants can become citizens if they go through the naturalization process in her eighth grade civics class. She decides she wants to become a naturalized citizen for her fourteenth birthday. 

**Which of the following qualifications does Kyleigh already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She can read, speak, write, and understand English.
She has lived in the United States for at least five years.
She meets the minimum age requirement.
She attended American public school.

assessment_items.question_content:
Jermaine was born in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. He moved to the United States when he was four years old, when his mom got lawful permanent resident status and has lived in Flint, Michigan for the past fifteen years.  He is applying to universities in the fall, and he wants to become a citizen before he starts college. He is a good student and has never gotten into any trouble with the law. He thinks he meets all the requirements to become a citizen. 

**Which of the following qualifications does Jermaine already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He has lived in the United States for the requisite time.
He has demonstrated that he is of “good moral character.”
He is a lawful permanent resident of the United States.
He has registered to vote in state and local elections.

assessment_items.question_content:
Antoine’s family moved to Portland, Oregon, from France when Antoine turned thirteen. Antoine did not take the move well and began acting out. When Antoine was nineteen, he and a few friends broke into an electronics store and stole nearly \$5000 worth of technology, including laptops and phones. He served two years in prison for the crime. After Antoine got out of prison, he wanted to become a citizen to make a better life for himself.

**Which of the following qualifications does Antoine already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He has lived in the United States for at least five years.
He meets the minimum age requirement.
He has shown that he is of “good moral character.”
He has served out his sentence before he applies.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rajesh moved his family from Bangladesh to Baton Rouge, Louisiana, seven years ago. He’s been a doctor at a Baton Rouge hospital for the last three years. He’s fluent in four languages, including English, and has taken to volunteering his time providing medical care for other immigrants who have recently made their way to the United States. He’s about to turn forty and thinks it’s time for him to go through the naturalization process. 

**Which of the following qualifications does Rajesh already have for citizenship?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He meets the minimum age requirement when he applies for citizenship.
He can speak, read, write, and understand English.
He has lived in the United States continuously for at least five years.
He shows that he believes in a commitment to constitutional principles.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nino emigrated from Georgia nine years ago. Last week, he applied for naturalization after he got fingerprinted for USCIS records. 

**What step does Nino need to take next in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: interview with an US Citizenship and Immigration official
apply for a Green Card to get lawful permanent residence
pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States
give up his Georgian citizenship to avoid a conflict of interest

assessment_items.question_content:
Ibrahim is originally from Sierra Leone, but he immigrated to the United States six years ago to fill a position at a major pharmaceutical company. He wants to become a naturalized citizen, but he doesn’t know where to start. 

**What step does Ibrahim need to take first in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: determine if he meets the requirements for naturalization
interview with an official from US Citizenship and Immigration Services
complete an application for naturalization as soon as possible
return to his home country to apply with US Citizenship and Immigration Services

assessment_items.question_content:
Zhiwei emigrated from Taiwan to the United States when he was seven years old. He has been in the United States for twelve years now. He went through the eligibility requirements for citizenship and found that he meets all of them. This includes the requirement that he lives in the United States for at least five years. 

**What step does Zhiwei need to take next in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: file an application for naturalization with US Citizenship and Immigration Services
apply for an immigrant visa and become a lawful permanent resident
renounce his Taiwanese citizenship to avoid having divided loyalties
take a literacy test to prove that he can speak, read, and understand English

assessment_items.question_content:
Pablo was born in Uruguay, but immigrated to the United States sixteen years ago to attend college. He has lived in the United States ever since. He started the naturalization process eighteen months ago. He filed his application for naturalization, had his interview with the USCIS official, and passed his literacy test and his civics test. 

**What step does Pablo need to take next in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States
apply for a United States passport
register to vote in his home state
renounce his Uruguayan citizenship

assessment_items.question_content:
Heather was born in Canada, but she has lived in the United States for the last twenty-three years. She decided that she wants to become a citizen this year so she can run for a congress in a few years. She’s determined that she is eligible for naturalization and applied for naturalization. 

**What step does Heather need to take next in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: interview with an official from US Citizenship and Immigration Services
submit proof of “good moral character” by showing a clean arrest record
become a lawful permanent resident by getting a Green Card
apply for dual citizenship to become eligible for naturalization

assessment_items.question_content:
Kristín moved to the United States from Iceland fourteen years ago after becoming a reporter for the *New York Times.* She thinks it’s time to become a US citizen, but doesn’t really know where to start. She looked at the eligibility requirements and thinks she meets most of them. 

**What step does Kristín need to take next in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: apply for naturalization with the US Citizenship and Immigration Services
leave the country to start the naturalization process from her country
report to a major port of entry to begin the naturalization process
join a major political party as a member to prove she is of “good moral character”

assessment_items.question_content:
Esther is originally from Kenya, but she’s lived in the United States for five years. She’s over eighteen and understands English. She applied for naturalization and met with an USCIS official. She passed both the literacy exam and the US citizenship exam. 

**What step does Esther need to take next in the naturalization process?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: pledge an oath of allegiance to the United States
register for the selective service
submit her intention for naturalization with her state
meet with her country’s ambassador
